siteEntity ptolemy.actor.CompositeActor ptolemy.actor.TypeOpaqueCompositeActor All Implemented Interfaces: Actor , Changeable , java.lang.Cloneable, Debuggable , DebugListener , Derivable , Executable , Instantiable , ModelErrorHandler , MoMLExportab

, Instantiable , ModelErrorHandler , MoMLExportable , Moveable , Nameable , java.io.Serializable, TypedActor public class TypeOpaqueCompositeActor extends CompositeActor implements TypedActor This is a composite actor whose ports have types, but the

a composite actor whose ports have types, but the actors inside are not required to be typed. This actor does not impose any type constraints between its ports, so any output ports of this actor have to have explicitly declared types. There is no

ve to have explicitly declared types. There is no mechanism for inferring the types of the output ports. Note that the ports of this actor cannot be linked on the inside to untyped relations, and by default, any relation created on the inside will

y default, any relation created on the inside will be untyped. The intended use of this actor is for scenarios where the inside structure is not part of the Ptolemy type system. For example, the inside structure may define a component that will be

ide structure may define a component that will be translated into executable code by a code generator. A subclass of this actor would typically include the code generator an a mechanism for executing the generating code. Since: Ptolemy II 4.1

ng the generating code. Since: Ptolemy II 4.1 Exp $ Version: $Id: TypeOpaqueCompositeActor.java,v 1.73 2004/04/13 05:12:39 cxh Author: Elaine Cheong and Edward A. Lee See Also: TypedCompositeActor , TypedIOPort , Serialized Form Accepted Ra
